description: Pirelli & C. S.p.A. manufactures and supplies tires for cars, motorcycles, and bicycles worldwide. It offers car tires under the P ZERO, Cinturato, Scorpion, Sottozero, Ice Zero, and Carrier brand names; motorcycle tires under the DIABLO Superbike, DIABLO Supercorsa, DIABLO Rain, DIABLO Wet, SCORPION MX, SCORPION Pro F.I.M., SCORPION XC, DIABLO Supercorsa SP, DIABLO Rosso Corsa, DIABLO Rosso III, ANGEL GT II, NIGHT DRAGON, MT 66 ROUTE, SCORPION Trail II, MT 60 RS, SCORPION Rally STR, DIABLO ROSSO Scooter, and ANGEL Scooter brands; motorsport tires under the P ZERO TROFEO R, SOTTOZERO, P7 Corsa, K, KM, RK, rain, and Slick brands; bike tires under the P Zero, Cinturato, and Scorpion brand names; and truck and bus tires. The company also provides Run Flat and Seal Inside specialties that enable cars to continue driving after a puncture; Pirelli Noise Cancelling System for reducing noise by rolling; and Elect, a line of tires for electric vehicles. It operates approximately 17,000 points of sale. Pirelli & C. S.p.A. was founded in 1872 and is headquartered in Milan, Italy.
ai_description:
Pirelli & C. S.p.A. is an Italian multinational company specializing in the design, manufacture, and sale of tires primarily for cars, motorcycles, and bicycles. Founded in 1872 and headquartered in Milan, Italy, the company operates globally across Europe, North America, Asia-Pacific, South America, Russia, and other markets. Pirelli’s core products include high-performance tires categorized under premium and prestige segments, with key product lines such as the P Zero, Cinturato, and Scorpion series, targeting light vehicles, SUVs, motorcycles, and bicycles. The company emphasizes technological innovation, including all-season and ultra-high-performance tires, with offerings designed for electric and hybrid vehicles as well as sustainable mobility solutions.

In recent strategic developments, Pirelli has focused on enhancing its position in the high-value tire segment, achieving around 210 new homologations for premium carmakers in 2025, particularly in rim sizes 19 inches and above. The company has expanded its product portfolio with new launches such as the fifth-generation P Zero, the new generation Cinturato summer tire for Europe, and the new Scorpion All Season SF3 tire for SUVs, designed with safety and sustainability features. Pirelli has also introduced the first standard production tire made with over 70% bio-based and recycled materials, underscoring its commitment to environmental sustainability. On the corporate front, Pirelli is exploring mergers and acquisitions focused on new materials and connectivity technologies to support its innovation and sustainability goals. It also renewed strategic partnerships, including the one with Luna Rossa for the 37th America's Cup in 2024, and has been active in developing digital and algorithmic capabilities for performance data integration in tires.

Pirelli operates through its main subsidiaries including Pirelli International Plc in the UK and maintains significant market penetration in both original equipment and replacement tire markets worldwide. The company’s strategic focus on research and development, premium market segments, and sustainable innovation positions it as a leader in the tire industry with a robust product portfolio and a forward-looking business model targeting both technological and environmental advancements. Recent shareholder agreements and capital moves have supported Pirelli’s growth trajectory and operational expansion through 2024 and into 2025.
address: Viale Piero e Alberto Pirelli n. 25
